extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won a chaotic sprint after Quick-Step sat back following too-early positioning at 5km, then perfectly timed final leadout inside last kilometer.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step deployed leadout at 5km, got swamped, deliberately sat back, then re-engaged inside 1km proving patience wins chaotic flat finishes.
UAE controlled inside 1.2km with Molano but wasted leadout too early; TRENTINO never pulled through showing pure wheel-sucking works in disorganized sprints.
Breakaway absorbed at intermediate sprint when peloton hit 40mph, proving teams use sprint points as calibration for swallowing early moves.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Second Vuelta win confirms full recovery from Poland crash; team now trusts him enough to abandon early position and re-commit
rider_signals.MOLANO Juan Sebastian: negative: UAE controlled at 1.2km with full leadout but he faded to fifth showing their train lacks final punch
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: Went for intermediate sprint to pad green jersey but finished second again showing speed without winning edge
rider_signals.MEEUS Jordi: positive: Fourth without major leadout suggests he can navigate chaos better than established names like Matthews
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Not even top 20 on pan-flat finish confirms he genuinely needs gradient, not just positioning, to compete
